entries.title: 1.2L Petrol

Timing chain stretch/phaser wear (80k-120k miles)
Electronic throttle body failure causing limp mode

commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi Mirage Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi Mirage's engine reliability varies significantly between the modern 3A92 1.2L and older 4G-series units. Oil consumption and timing chain issues dominate 3A92 replacement enquiries, while 4G15/4G92 owners report head gasket and ignition failures. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Oil Consumption - Why Mitsubishi Mirage 3A92 Engines Burn Oil Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Mirage 1.2 ES, GL, LS, Attraze (2012-2024, 3A92 & 3G83)
problems.rootCause: Weak factory piston ring design allows oil to pass into combustion chamber under normal operating conditions.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace piston rings, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before cylinder scoring; 20k-40k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded piston rings, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution; recommended for vehicles worth retaining

problems.recommendation: On a modern Mirage valued at £2,000-£4,000, a full rebuilt engine at £800-£1,900 often represents better long-term value than repeated top-end repairs. We recommend replacement once oil consumption exceeds 1L per 1,000 miles.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 3A92 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Mirage

problems.h4: Timing Chain Failure - Why Mitsubishi Mirage Timing Chains Stretch
problems.rootCause: Chain tensioner wear and phaser mechanism fatigue cause slack, leading to rattling noise and potential valve timing loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioner, guides, phasers; reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative fix if caught before damage; 60k-80k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded timing components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if chain has jumped or caused valve damage

problems.recommendation: If you hear a rattling noise on cold start but the engine still runs smoothly, a timing chain kit replacement at £600-£1,100 is cost-effective prevention. If the chain has jumped, replacement is the only safe option.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 3A92 timing chain replacement Mitsubishi Mirage

problems.h4: Head Gasket Failure - Why Mitsubishi Mirage 4G15 Engines Overheat
problems.rootCause: Age-related gasket degradation combined with cooling system neglect leads to coolant/oil mixing and compression loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace gasket, machine head if warped, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block is undamaged; 40k-60k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4G15 with new gasket set, pressure tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if block is scored or head is severely warped

problems.recommendation: For a classic Mirage 1.5 valued at £800-£1,500, a specialist head gasket repair at £500-£900 often makes financial sense. However, if the block shows scoring, a rebuilt unit at £650-£1,400 provides better long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4G15 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Mirage

problems.h4: Ignition Coil Failure - Why Mitsubishi Mirage Engines Misfire
problems.rootCause: Age-related insulation breakdown in coil packs causes intermittent spark loss, leading to rough running and catalytic converter damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnose faulty coil(s), replace with OEM-spec units, clear fault codes
repairOptions.longevity: Immediate fix; coils typically last 40k-60k miles after replacement

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new ignition system, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only if misfire has caused severe internal damage (rare)

problems.recommendation: Ignition coil failure is almost always a simple, affordable repair. Replace faulty coils at £150-£350 before considering engine replacement - unless misfire has caused catalytic converter damage or piston washdown.
problems.cta: Find ignition coil specialists for your Mitsubishi Mirage

problems.h4: GDI Carbon Build-up - Why Mitsubishi Mirage 4G93 Engines Lose Power
problems.rootCause: Direct injection design deposits carbon on inlet valves (no fuel wash), restricting airflow and causing rough idle, power loss, and eventual failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove manifold, walnut blast or chemical clean inlet valves, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught before severe damage; 30k-50k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4G93 with updated components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if carbon has caused valve damage or piston scoring

problems.recommendation: For GDI owners, regular inlet cleaning every 30k miles is the best prevention. If power loss is already severe, a rebuilt 4G93 at £1,100-£2,200 often costs less than repeated decarbonising plus associated repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4G93 GDI engine prices for your Mitsubishi Mirage

problems.h4: Electronic Throttle Failure - Why Modern Mirage Engines Enter Limp Mode
problems.rootCause: Throttle position sensor wear or motor failure in electronic throttle body causes erratic response, triggering ECU limp mode for safety.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove old throttle body, fit new or reconditioned unit, perform ECU adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Immediate fix; new units typically last 60k+ miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new throttle system, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only if throttle failure caused secondary damage (rare)

problems.recommendation: Electronic throttle failure is almost always resolved by throttle body replacement at £350-£700. Only consider full engine replacement if the failure caused severe overheating or secondary damage - which is uncommon.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 3A92 throttle body replacement Mitsubishi Mirage

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi Mirage
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're dealing with oil consumption on a modern 1.2 ES or head gasket issues on a classic 1.5 GLX, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mitsubishi Mirage specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi Mirage Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi Mirage and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mitsubishi Mirage Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Mitsubishi Mirage is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Mitsubishi Mirage 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The smallest displacement option appeared in early H81A chassis Mirages (2012-2017), offering maximum fuel economy for urban driving. The 4A91 unit shares architecture with larger 3A92 engines but uses a simpler timing chain system.
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Mirage 1.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Mirage 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The dominant engine for modern Mirages (2012-present), the 1.2L 3-cylinder 3A92/3G83 powers most UK-spec city cars. Known for affordability but watch for oil consumption and timing chain issues on higher-mileage examples.
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Mirage 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Mirage 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The entry-level engine for classic Mirages (1990-2002), the 1.3L 4G13 SOHC unit powered base GL and GS trims. Simple, reliable design but age-related ignition and cooling system failures are common.
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Mirage 1.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Mirage 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The high-volume workhorse of the 1996-2002 Mirage range, the 1.5L 4G15 SOHC engine powered GLX and Colt-based variants. Known for durability but head gasket and timing belt failures require attention.
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Mirage 1.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Mirage 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The versatile 1.6L 4G92 DOHC engine powered standard GLXi models and the legendary Asti RX Version R with MIVEC. Watch for hydraulic lash adjuster noise and ignition coil issues on high-mileage examples.
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Mirage 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Mirage 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The flagship 1.8L 4G93 GDI engine featured early direct injection technology for improved efficiency. Carbon build-up on inlet valves is the dominant failure mode in UK driving conditions.
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Mirage 1.8L petrol engine prices

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Mitsubishi Mirage needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Mitsubishi Mirage specialists.

engineYears.h2: Mitsubishi Mirage Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Mitsubishi Mirage production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: First UK-market Mirages featured simple SOHC 1.3L and 1.5L engines on the CA chassis platform.
keyChanges: Introduction of Mirage nameplate to UK market
CA chassis with front-wheel drive layout
SOHC 4G13 and 4G15 engines standard across range
knownFor: Simple, reliable SOHC design
Age-related ignition and cooling system failures
Strong parts availability from breakers

faq.h2: Mitsubishi Mirage Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: What engines fit a Mitsubishi Mirage?
items.answer: Compatibility depends entirely on your Mirage generation. Modern 2012+ models use 3A92/3G83 1.2L engines; classic 1990s-2000s models use 4G13/4G15/4G92/4G93 series. Never assume interchangeability between generations.
keyPoints: Modern Mirages (2012+) exclusively use 3A92 or 3G83 1.2L engines
Classic Mirages (1990-2002) use 4G13, 4G15, 4G92 or 4G93 engines
Engine mounts, ECU and transmission interfaces differ completely between generations
Always verify your engine code via registration check before ordering
items.cta: Check your Mirage engine compatibility instantly

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Mitsubishi Mirage engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt Mirage engine prices range from £550 for classic 4G13 units to £2,500 for performance 4G92 MIVEC engines. Modern 3A92 replacements typically cost £800-£1,900 supply-only.
keyPoints: Classic 4G13/4G15 engines: £550-£1,400 rebuilt supply-only
Modern 3A92/3G83 engines: £800-£1,900 rebuilt supply-only
Performance 4G92 MIVEC: £1,400-£2,500 rebuilt supply-only
Supply & fit labour adds £300-£600 depending on location
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Mirage engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: What causes Mitsubishi Mirage 1.2 oil consumption?
items.answer: Weak factory piston rings on the 3A92 engine allow oil to enter the combustion chamber, causing excessive consumption typically from 40,000-80,000 miles.
keyPoints: Factory piston ring design is the root cause on 3A92 engines
Consumption worsens with mileage and hard driving
Rebuilt engines with upgraded rings solve the issue permanently
Monitor oil level weekly if consumption exceeds 1L per 1,000 miles
items.cta: Get quotes for 3A92 rebuilt engines with upgraded piston rings

items.question: Is the Mitsubishi Mirage 3A92 timing chain reliable?
items.answer: The 3A92 timing chain can stretch or suffer phaser wear around 80,000-120,000 miles despite Mitsubishi's "lifetime" claims, leading to rattling noise and potential valve timing loss.
keyPoints: Chain tensioner and phaser wear cause slack over time
Rattling noise on cold start is the primary warning sign
Preventative chain kit replacement costs £600-£1,100 specialist
Full engine replacement recommended if chain has jumped
items.cta: Compare timing chain replacement vs rebuilt engine costs

items.question: Will a 4G92 engine fit my 1.5 Mirage?
items.answer: No - the 4G92 1.6L engine will not directly fit a 4G15 1.5L Mirage without extensive modification to mounts, wiring, ECU and transmission.
keyPoints: Engine mounts, wiring looms and ECUs are generation-specific
Transmission bellhousing patterns differ between 4G15 and 4G92
MIVEC engine swaps require custom fabrication and tuning
For reliability, fit the correct engine code for your chassis
items.cta: Find the correct replacement engine for your Mirage variant

items.question: How long should a Mitsubishi Mirage engine last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance, classic 4G15 engines often exceed 150,000 miles; modern 3A92 units typically reach 140,000-170,000 miles before major issues arise.
keyPoints: 4G15 engines known for longevity with regular oil changes
3A92 engines prone to oil consumption from 40k miles
Timing chain maintenance critical for 3A92 beyond 80k miles
Service history significantly impacts actual lifespan
items.cta: Check rebuilt engine prices for your Mirage's expected replacement window

items.question: Do you warranty Mitsubishi Mirage replacement engines?
items.answer: Yes - every engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists, covering parts and labour for manufacturing defects.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard
Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
items.cta: Compare warranty terms across UK Mirage engine suppliers

items.question: Can I fit a Mitsubishi Mirage engine myself?
items.answer: While technically possible for experienced DIYers, professional fitting is strongly recommended to preserve warranty coverage and ensure correct installation of complex modern systems.
keyPoints: Modern Mirages have complex electronics requiring specialist tools
Incorrect installation can void engine warranty
Supply & fit service adds £300-£600 but preserves warranty
Professional fitting includes post-installation diagnostics
items.cta: Find supply & fit specialists for your Mitsubishi Mirage

items.question: Where can I buy a Mitsubishi Mirage engine in the UK?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK specialists supplying rebuilt, reconditioned and used Mirage engines for all generations and engine codes.
keyPoints: Network includes 100+ vetted UK engine specialists
Compare prices, warranty terms and supply & fit options side-by-side
All suppliers verified for Mirage engine expertise
Instant quotes based on your registration-verified details
items.cta: Get free quotes from UK Mitsubishi Mirage engine specialists

items.question: What causes Mitsubishi Mirage electronic throttle failure?
items.answer: Wear in the throttle position sensor or motor within the electronic throttle body causes erratic response, triggering ECU limp mode on modern 3A92-equipped Mirages.
keyPoints: Throttle position sensor wear is the most common cause
Symptoms include poor throttle response, stalling, limp mode
Replacement throttle body costs £350-£700 fitted specialist
Rebuilt engine replacement only needed if secondary damage occurred
items.cta: Get quotes for 3A92 throttle body or full engine replacement
